t_ch: 你是忆庭的忆者…？
t_en: You're the Garden of Recollection's Memokeeper...?

t_ch: 原始梦境是什么…
t_en: What is the Primal Dreamscape...?

t_ch: 流萤…她逃出来了吗？
t_en: Firefly... Did she make it out?

t_ch: 呵呵，你很关心那个小姑娘啊。也是，即便在「死亡」面前，她也竭尽所能想要保护你的安全…连我都有些心动了呢。
t_en: Heheh, you're quite concerned about that young lady. That's fair — she was hell-bent on assuring your safety even at the brink of death... Started to tug a little at my heartstrings, even.

t_ch: 我知道你脑袋里有许多问号，别心急。在那之前，先向担心你的伙伴们报个平安吧。
t_en: I know you've many questions, so please compose yourself. Before that, you should let your friends know you're safe.

t_ch: 某人的宝物
t_en: Someone's treasure

t_ch: （唔…这是哪儿？）
t_en: (Ugh... Where's this?)

t_ch: （这里是…贝洛伯格下层区的诊所？为什么空无一人？）
t_en: (Is this... the clinic in the Belobog Underworld? Why is it empty?)

t_ch: 我怎么在这儿？
t_en: How did I end up here?

t_ch: （这是我梦境的一部分——这个梦的舞台就是贝洛伯格。）
t_en: (This is part of my Dreamscape — Belobog is the setting for this dream.)

t_ch: 人呢？人都哪去了？
t_en: Where is everyone?

t_ch: （这是个惊喜，我们稍后再揭晓。但你要明白——在梦中，一切皆有可能。）
t_en: (This is a surprise that will be revealed later. You have to remember — in a dream, anything is possible.)

t_ch: 我现在该做什么？
t_en: What should I do now?

t_ch: （别急，你应该先熟悉下梦中梦的感觉。）
t_en: (No need to be hasty, now. You should take a moment to get used to the feeling of being in a dream within a dream.)

t_ch: （这是…又一张纸条？）
t_en: (Is this... another note?)

t_ch: 你什么意思？
t_en: What do you mean by this?

t_ch: （不知道啊，我做梦时完全没来过这个地方。）
t_en: (I have no idea. I didn't come here when I had this dream.)

t_ch: 似曾相识的桥段…
t_en: I think I've had this dream before too...

t_ch: （哦，是吗？如有雷同，不胜荣幸！）
t_en: (Oh, really? Well you know what they say — great minds dream alike!)

t_ch: 好熟悉的谏言…
t_en: This all feels so familiar...

t_ch: （人会在梦里看到任何东西都是很正常的啦。）
t_en: (That's the thing about dreams — anything can feel familiar.)

t_ch: （别在意这些细节了——要我说，你该出门看看了，小心别被吓到！）
t_en: (Stop nitpicking the details. If you ask me, I'd say you should take a look outside. But don't get frightened by what you see!)
(What...)

t_ch: （…这是什么情况？！）
t_en: (...What's happening!?)

t_ch: 这是什么情况？！
t_en: What's happening!?

t_ch: 怎么全是垃圾桶？
t_en: Why are there trashcans everywhere?

t_ch: 您终于来了……
t_en: You're finally here...

t_ch: 幸会，深潜于梦中之人啊……
t_en: Nice to meet you, dream diver...

t_ch: 我名为莎塔娜——想和您谈一个条件。
t_en: My name is Shatana, and I have a deal to offer you.

t_ch: 条件？什么条件？
t_en: Deal? What deal?

t_ch: 您…曾听说过「垃圾之王」塔塔洛夫吗？
t_en: Have you heard of... Tatalov the Garbage King?

t_ch: 莎塔娜？你和娜塔莎什么关系？
t_en: Shatana? Are you related to Natasha?

t_ch: 娜塔莎…是谁？想必我们未曾谋面。
t_en: Natasha who? I don't recall meeting such a person.

t_ch: 让我们言归正传吧——您…曾听说过「垃圾之王」塔塔洛夫吗？
t_en: Let's get back to the question... Have you heard of Tatalov the Garbage King?

t_ch: 你这台词好像也挺耳熟啊…
t_en: That line of yours sounds eerily familiar...

t_ch: 恐怕…这就是所谓的既视感……
t_en: You're... probably just experiencing some déjà vu...

t_ch: 让我们言归正传吧——您…曾听说过「垃圾之王」塔塔洛夫吗？
t_en: Let's get back to the question... Have you heard of Tatalov the Garbage King?

t_ch: 祂是我等的王，也是世间一切废料的牧人。他立于王座上，扶持谦卑的桶，压制强暴的桶；祂是贝洛伯格的墙基，稳固我等的桶盖，坚直我等的桶身……
t_en: He's our king, as well as the manager of all the waste in this world. Perched on his throne, he uplifts trashcans that are humble and suppresses those that are violent. He's the cornerstone of Belobog, the lid that secures us, the barrel that reinforces us.

t_ch: 然而，当天外之物随寒潮降临此地，他的双眼便被蒙蔽了——他不再倾听呼告，使穷苦之桶遭受欺凌，令忠诚之桶平白蒙羞……
t_en: However, since the thing from beyond the sky arrived here along with the Eternal Freeze, Tatalov's eyes have been blinded. He stopped listening to our calls, leaving the impoverished to be bullied and his loyal servants to be degraded...

t_ch: 现在，是时候了。我等不堪受辱，誓要举起天火的大旗，令垃圾之王改邪归正……
t_en: A change was overdue, as the humiliation was too much for us. We intended to help Tatalov mend his ways under the banner of the Skyfire...

t_ch: 我等不堪受辱，誓要举起天火的大旗，令垃圾之王改邪归正。但我们不敌那邪恶的王…他散布恶毒的谣言，分化众桶，令{RUBY_B#心}垃圾袋{RUBY_E#}不再能彼此感应……
t_en: The humiliation was too much for us. We intended to help Garbage King mend his ways under the banner of the Skyfire... But we were no match for the sinister king, who spread vicious rumors among the trashcans to divide us against each other. As a result, we could no longer communicate with one another using {RUBY_B#Heart}trashbags{RUBY_E#}...

t_ch: …我请求您，使此地的垃圾袋恢复宁静，再度联结众桶吧——如此一来，便能向那伪王发起反击。
t_en: ...I implore your help to restore peace to the trash bags, so that the trashcans here may be united once more. Then, we'll be able to launch a counterattack against that undeserving king.

t_ch: 你的意思是，要我团结你们？
t_en: Are you saying you want me to rally you all together?

t_ch: （看来她确实是这个意思……）
t_en: (I think that's what she means...)

t_ch: 我听不懂你说的谜语…
t_en: You're speaking in riddles...

t_ch: （她应该是想说，只有你能团结这群，呃，垃圾桶，然后带领他们打倒邪恶的坏人…吧。）
t_en: (I think she's trying to say that you're the only one who can unite these, uhm, trashcans and lead their battle against the evil king... or something like that.)

t_ch: 那么，契约便成立了。
t_en: Well, that seals the deal.

t_ch: 我应该做什么？
t_en: What do I need to do?

t_ch: 契约？什么契约？
t_en: Deal? What deal?

t_ch: 我还没同意呢！
t_en: But I haven't agreed to anything!

t_ch: （爱德华医生说他来不及做那么多分支了，你就接受吧。）
t_en: (Dr. Edward said he didn't have the time to make any alternative storylines. Just accept it.)

t_ch: 您只需完成一件事——<color=#dbc291ff>帮助垃圾桶们从烦扰中解脱，取得三个「信任的证明」。</color>
t_en: You have one mission — <color=#dbc291ff>help the trashcans with their troubles and collect three Proofs of Trust</color>.

t_ch: 如此一来，您便能成为新王，率领我等向伪王发起抗争。去吧，深潜于梦中之人啊…使命在召唤着你。
t_en: Once you've accomplished that, you'll become our new king and lead our fight against the evil king. Off you go, dream diver... Your mission is calling for you.

t_ch: （这都什么跟什么……）
t_en: (I'm confused...)

t_ch: 去吧，异乡的来访者…使命在召唤着你。
t_en: Off you go, dream diver... Your mission is calling for you.

t_ch: （总算拿到三个「信任的证明」了…回去交差吧。）
t_en: (I've finally collected three Proofs of Trust... Let's go back and deliver them.)

t_ch: 嗯，看来您已经完成了契约……
t_en: Excellent. Looks like you've held up your side of the deal...

t_ch: 这…您竟然汇集了全部「信任的证明」，这份决心不容小觑……
t_en: Impressive... It's hard to believe you've collected all the Proofs of Trust. Your determination is truly admirable...

t_ch: …这一天终于来了。那么，出发吧——我会护送您前往王座「造物之柱」，见证我等一族的未来……
t_en: ...I've waited so long for this day. Here, I'll escort you to your throne, the Pillars of Creation, where you'll witness the future of my people...

t_ch: 祝您成功。
t_en: I wish you success.

t_ch: （这是到决战之地了吗…心情好复杂啊……）
t_en: (Is this where the final battle will take place... Not sure how I feel about this...)

t_ch: （这也太惨烈了吧……）
t_en: (Such a tragic scene...)

t_ch: （…他们不是说垃圾之王在这儿等我吗？）
t_en: (...Didn't they say the Garbage King would wait for me here?)

t_ch: （我是不是被耍了…？）
t_en: (Have I been set up?)

t_ch: 深不见底的垃圾箱里有一个奇怪的垃圾袋，你注意到袋上的「封印」——几条鱼骨，将袋口牢牢卡住。
t_en: A strange trash bag sits in the bottomless trashcan. You notice the "seal" on the bag — A few fish bones have clipped the mouth of the bag shut.

t_ch: 摘除封印。
t_en: Remove the seal.

t_ch: 啪！一声巨响，一股青烟从袋中徐徐升起，在空中拧成一团，缓缓变成了一个巨大的...垃圾桶。
t_en: Bam! Green smoke wafts up from within the bag together with a loud noise. It twists and congeals in the air, slowly becoming a giant... trashcan.

t_ch: 终于——我重获自由了！我是「垃垃撕圾」，一个无比邪恶的桶。塔塔洛夫用卑劣的手段击败我，把我封印进这深邃的垃圾袋。
t_en: Finally — I am free! I am "Garboski," a can of unmatched evil. Tatalov used despicable means to defeat me and sealed me in this deep and cramped trash bag.

t_ch: 最开始的五百年，我发誓如果有桶来解救我，我必赐给他无穷的财富。第二个五百年，我发誓如果有桶来解救我，我必赐给他无尽的垃圾。
t_en: For the first five hundred years of my imprisonment, I swore an oath that if a can came to save me, I would reward them with endless wealth. Then, for the next five hundred years, I swore that if a can saved me, I would reward them with endless trash.

t_ch: 但时间过去一万年——整整二十个五百年——始终没有桶来救我！于是我发誓如果有桶来解救我，我必赐给他无痛的死亡。
t_en: However, ten thousand years have passed. That's <unbreak>20</unbreak> five-hundred-year periods, but no cans came! Therefore, I swore that if a can came to save me, I would reward them with a painless death.

t_ch: ——你来了，你便是一定要死的。
t_en: — You've come, and so you now must die.

t_ch: 这不公平，我救了你！
t_en: This is not fair! I saved you!

t_ch: 没错，所以我会给予你最后的慈悲，选择自己死法的权利。
t_en: Correct. I will now offer you a final mercy. You get to choose how you wish to die.

t_ch: 我要像战士一样死去！
t_en: I wish to die as a warrior!

t_ch: 哦？从来没有桶敢向我发起决斗，我承认你的英勇…也许我们没有必要进行无谓的争斗。
t_en: Oh? No trashcan has ever dared to challenge me to a duel. I acknowledge your bravery... Perhaps there's no need to conduct a pointless battle.

t_ch: 何不与我一起对抗塔塔洛夫？
t_en: Why not stand with me against Tatalov?

t_ch: 数万年的囚禁早就磨平了我的桶边，我已风光不再，只想以一个普通垃圾桶的身份度过余生。再见了，陌生的人。
t_en: Countless years of captivity have whittled flat my edges. My prime is long past, never to return. I only wish to pass the rest of my life as a normal trashcan. Farewell, stranger.

t_ch: 我不信你会被关进垃圾袋里。
t_en: I don't believe you were sealed in the trash bag.

t_ch: 没错…塔塔洛夫就是用这样的把戏耍了我：「你这样巨大的身躯，垃圾袋怎么容得下你呢？」于是我当着他的面钻进了垃圾袋，就这样遭到封印。
t_en: Ah, yes, Tatalov used the same trick against me. "With such a massive body like yours, how can a bag fit you?" And so I slipped into a trash bag before him and found myself sealed.

t_ch: 再邪恶的桶也懂得吸取教训，你这招已经无效了！现在，感受我被囚禁了一万年——整整二十个五百年——的愤怒吧！
t_en: Even the vilest trashcan is capable of learning to heed a lesson. Your tactics are ineffective against me now! Now, feel my ten thousand years — five hundred years times twenty — of fury!

t_ch: 可我是人，不是垃圾桶。
t_en: But I'm a person, not a trashcan.

t_ch: …你说得没错，你不是我期待中的救主，因此你没有背叛我的期待。我应该感谢你才对。走吧，我不会取你的性命，也仅止于此了。
t_en: You're right. You are not the savior that I expected. And because you haven't betrayed my expectations... I should thank you instead. Go. I won't take your life, but that's the limit of my mercy.

t_ch: 何不与我一起对抗塔塔洛夫？
t_en: Why not stand with me against Tatalov?

t_ch: 数万年的囚禁早就磨平了我的桶边，我已风光不再，只想以一个普通垃圾桶的身份度过余生。再见了，陌生的人。
t_en: Countless years of captivity have whittled flat my edges. My prime is long past, never to return. I only wish to pass the rest of my life as a normal trashcan. Farewell, stranger.

t_ch: 你解决了被封印的恶魔，垃垃撕圾？他那么恐怖，那么强大…但你却战胜了他——何其伟大！
t_en: You dealt with the sealed demon, Garboski? He is so powerful and so terrifying, but you defeated him... How magnificent of you!

t_ch: 你赢得了我，还有其他桶的信任。希望此物可以表达我们对你的承认。
t_en: You've won me over, along with many others. This item carries our high regard for you.

t_ch: 想不到啊，你很强大，我承认你的英勇…也许我们没有必要进行无谓的争斗。
t_en: I didn't expect you to be so strong. I acknowledge your bravery... Perhaps there's no need to conduct a pointless battle.

t_ch: 何不与我一起对抗塔塔洛夫？
t_en: Why not stand with me against Tatalov?

t_ch: 数万年的囚禁早就磨平了我的桶边，我已风光不再，只想以一个普通垃圾桶的身份度过余生。再见了，陌生的人。
t_en: Countless years of captivity have whittled flat my edges. My prime is long past, never to return. I only wish to pass the rest of my life as a normal trashcan. Farewell, stranger.

t_ch: 这是一个普通的垃圾桶。多么神奇，在一座由垃圾桶构成的王国里，居然存在着普通的垃圾桶…机会难得，你打算对它做点什么？
t_en: This is a normal-looking trashcan. How magical! A kingdom of trashcans actually has an ordinary trashcan... This is a rare opportunity. Do you wish to do something to it?

t_ch: 放入垃圾。
t_en: Insert trash.

t_ch: 当然，这就是垃圾桶的工作。你随便找了点什么扔进它漆黑而深不见底的洞口，一阵巨响随之传来……
t_en: Of course. That's the job of a trashcan. You chuck whatever random things you have on hand into its pitch-black and bottomless void. A series of loud sounds thundered back.

t_ch: 取出垃圾。
t_en: Remove some trash.

t_ch: 当然，这就是垃圾桶的价值。你用手指触碰它的桶壁，一阵巨响随之传来……
t_en: Of course. That's the value of a trashcan. When you used your finger to touch its sides, a burst of loud sounds followed.

t_ch: 踢它一脚。
t_en: Give it a kick.

t_ch: …这显然是不文明的，换作平时，你恐怕得支付一个「崇高道德的赞许」——但这里是梦境，所以管他呢，你一脚踢在桶壁上，一阵巨响随之传来……
t_en: ...Evidently, this is not civilized behavior. In regular circumstances, you'd probably have to give up a Praise of High Morals... However, this is the Dreamscape, so who cares? You feel your foot impact the can with a loud bam...

t_ch: 当你重新睁开眼时，垃圾桶已不再是垃圾桶，而是一只长着双手与双腿的...垃圾桶。
t_en: When you open your eyes again, the trashcan is no longer just a trashcan. It's now a trashcan... with arms and legs.

t_ch: 感谢你，美丽又陌生的人。我是垃圾桶王子，我的父亲被塔塔洛夫暗害，我也被邪术变成了垃圾桶。是你救了我，将我从囚禁中解放出来。
t_en: Thank you, beautiful stranger. I am the Trashcan Prince, and my father was assassinated by Tatalov, and I was cursed into becoming a trashcan. You saved me from internment.

t_ch: 我愿献上我的一切——身体、灵魂、生命、垃圾袋——只为报答你的恩情！
t_en: I shall offer everything I have — my body, my soul, my life, even my trash bags — just to repay your kindness.

t_ch: 你说话好像纯美骑士团…
t_en: You talk like a Knight of Beauty...

t_ch: 的确有许多桶夸赞我的美貌，但我不清楚你所说的名号。在这偌大的王国里，唯一的骑士团是「金钢镚骑士团」，每名成员都是十足高尚的桶。
t_en: There are many cans who compliment my beauty, but I am not familiar with the name of which you just spoke. The only knightly order in this kingdom is the "Knights of Loose Change," each of whom is a noble and respectable can of the highest order.

t_ch: 那就和我一起反抗塔塔洛夫。
t_en: Then stand with me against Tatalov.

t_ch: 我愿意。我会与你一同点燃反抗的狼烟，将王国从危难中拯救。
t_en: I will. I will stand with you and ignite the rising smoke of rebellion until the kingdom is saved from absolute peril.

t_ch: 你已得到了我的信任，希望你能将此物连带着我的心一并收下。我将为你而战，伟大的灵魂。
t_en: You have earned my confidence. I wish for you to accept this item and my heart as a token of my sincere gratitude. I will fight for you, O glorious soul.

t_ch: 到底谁才能配得上？
t_en: Then who is worthy of it?

t_ch: 只有我才该得到它！
t_en: Only I should have it!

t_ch: 这件事还需要讨论…
t_en: This requires further discussion...

t_ch: 三个垃圾桶围在一起，似乎在为什么事争论不休……
t_en: Three trashcans stand around each other, arguing ceaselessly about a certain matter...

t_ch: 异乡人，这里有一桩纠纷等待着你公正的判决。
t_en: Outlander, a dispute awaits your fair judgment.

t_ch: 这是传说中的金垃圾，权力与地位的象征。只有最具资格的桶才能占有它。我们都觉得自己才是那个桶，因此争吵不休。
t_en: This is the legendary golden trash, a symbol of power and status. Only the most qualified trashcan may lay claim to it. All of us feel that we are that trashcan, and so we keep fighting for it.

t_ch: 于是我们决定让一位眼光独到，且态度公正的旁观者代为评判，决定金垃圾的归属，为长久的争吵画上一个句号。
t_en: So, we have decided that an observer possessing unique and just insight should be the judge for this matter and decide to whom the reward will belong, and bring a conclusive end to this long dispute.

t_ch: 你若将金垃圾赐予我，我便许诺你无穷的财富，多到足以填满最大的桶。
t_en: If you give the golden trash to me, I will pledge you endless wealth, more than enough to fill the biggest trashcan there is.

t_ch: 你若将金垃圾赐予我，我便许诺你无穷的智慧，胜过任何一袋满溢的垃圾。
t_en: If you give the golden trash to me, I will grant you endless knowledge, more than any full bag of trashcan provide.

t_ch: 你若将金垃圾赐予我，我便许诺你世上最美丽的垃圾桶——也就是我——与你结伴而立，共同面对风吹雨打。
t_en: If you bestow upon me this golden trash, I shall promise you the most beautiful trashcan in the world — myself. I will stand beside you as a partner beyond peer and face all your trials and triumphs by your side.

t_ch: 那么…你要如何选择呢？
t_en: So... what is your choice?

t_ch: 将金垃圾切成三份平均分配。
t_en: Split golden trash into three and distribute evenly.

t_ch: 众目睽睽之下，你对金垃圾做出了完美的均分，就连钟表披萨的每一块都不一定如此均等。
t_en: Amidst the watchful gaze of the three, you perfectly split the golden trash. Not even a Clockie Pizza can be divided this evenly.

t_ch: 困扰了我们许久的难题，竟然就这样解决了……
t_en: The conundrum that has stymied us for so long... fixed just like that?

t_ch: 那到底谁才是最具资格的桶…不，那已经不重要了。
t_en: Then who is the most qualified can... No, that no longer mattered.

t_ch: 这位异乡人一定是想让我们明白，眼下的纷争是多么可笑…您用行动证明了自己。此物象征着我们的追随与憧憬，希望您能收下！
t_en: The outlander must be trying to help us understand how foolish this dispute of ours was... You have proven yourself with your actions. This signifies our loyalty and longing. We wish for you to have it.

t_ch: 我才是唯一配得上它的人！
t_en: I am the only one worthy of it!

t_ch: 众目睽睽之下，你将金垃圾据为己有。确实，这些小小的垃圾桶人…有哪个能像你一样高大、挺拔？
t_en: Under the watchful eyes of the three, you claim the golden trash as your own. Indeed, among these small trashcans, is there anyone who stands as tall and upright as you do?

t_ch: 困扰了我们许久的难题，竟然就这样解决了……
t_en: The conundrum that has stymied us for so long... fixed just like that?

t_ch: 那到底谁才是最具资格的桶…不，那已经不重要了。
t_en: Then who is the most qualified can... No, that no longer mattered.

t_ch: 这位异乡人一定是想让我们明白，眼下的纷争是多么可笑…您用行动证明了自己。此物象征着我们的追随与憧憬，希望您能收下！
t_en: The outlander must be trying to help us understand how foolish this dispute of ours was... You have proven yourself with your actions. This signifies our loyalty and longing. We wish for you to have it.

t_ch: 到底该怎么打开？
t_en: How can this be opened?

t_ch: 一定有什么办法的...
t_en: There has to be a way...

t_ch: 用桶盖狠狠地砸！
t_en: Smash it with the lid!

t_ch: 还是智取，智取…
t_en: Gotta work smarter, smarter...

t_ch: 我们受够了塔塔洛夫的摧残！要从他的宝库里拿回自己的东西，塔塔洛夫贪婪、暴戾，抢走了大家赖以生计的物资，我们必须打开这道门。
t_en: We've had enough of Tatalov's oppression! To retrieve our belongings from his treasure trove, the resources vital to our livelihoods seized by Tatalov's greed and tyranny, we must open this door.

t_ch: 推一下门。
t_en: Push the door.

t_ch: 踢门一脚。
t_en: Kick the door.

t_ch: 芝麻开门！
t_en: Open sesame!

t_ch: 奇怪，你还什么都没做，大门就缓缓打开了——原来如此，这是一道自动感应门，只是感应不了垃圾桶。
t_en: Strange, you haven't done anything, but the door opens slowly — it turns out that this is an automatic sensor door. It just can't sense trashcans.

t_ch: 大门打开了…传闻是真的，你是注定要击败塔塔洛夫的救世主！垃圾之神派你来将暴君的财宝散给我们！
t_en: The door is open... The legends are true! You are the savior destined to defeat Tatalov! The God of Trash sent you to return the tyrant's treasures to us!

t_ch: 啊，我从没感觉像现在这样畅快过！等你向塔塔洛夫掀起反旗时，我和其他受压迫的桶必跟随你的左右！
t_en: Ah, I've never felt so exhilarated before! When you raise the banner of rebellion against Tatalov, I and the other oppressed cans shall stand by your side!

t_ch: 你看着垃圾桶们兴奋地挤进「宝库」，撕扯开一袋又一袋垃圾，将它们尽数倒进自己的桶身……
t_en: You watch the trashcans excitedly crowd into the "treasure trove," tearing open bag after bag of garbage and pouring the contents into their torso...

t_ch: 九万七千八百六十三…
t_en: Ninety-seven thousand eight hundred sixty-three...

t_ch: 什么时候才是个头啊…
t_en: When will this come to an end...

t_ch: 这个垃圾桶看起来很安静。他的桶盖已经掉漆发白。他正一粒一粒地数着路边的垃圾，时而叹气、时而挠头，似乎正尽力记住一个天文数字。
t_en: This particular trashcan stands quietly. Its lid has chipped and faded paint. It is meticulously counting the roadside trash, occasionally sighing or scratching its head, as if struggling to memorize an astronomical number.

t_ch: …你也是来嘲笑我的吗？又或者是来寻求智慧的答案？
t_en: ...Are you here to mock me too? Or are you seeking wise answers?

t_ch: 你为什么要数垃圾？
t_en: Why are you counting trash?

t_ch: 说来话长。很久以前，我触怒了塔塔洛夫。我对他说，再稳固的统治也有衰亡的一天，就像再多的垃圾也可以被数尽，不知是哪半句话让他非常恼火……
t_en: It's a long story. A long, long time ago, I angered Tatalov. I told him that even the most stable of nations has their day of decay, and even all the trash in the stars can eventually be counted. It is unclear which part of the sentence infuriated him...

t_ch: 作为惩罚，他将我发配至此，要我数尽这座城中所有的垃圾。在那之前，我将永远不能发表自己的看法。
t_en: ...As punishment, he assigned me this role, demanding that I count all the trash in the city. Before then, I am to never issue forth my opinions.

t_ch: 我需要「信任的证明」。
t_en: I need "Proof of Trust."

t_ch: 哦…很少有桶会如此开门见山，我确实拥有「信任的证明」，但如今它已经遗失在了垃圾山中。
t_en: Oh... it's rare for a can to be so straightforward. I do possess the "Proof of Trust," but now it's lost in the mountain of trash.

t_ch: 如果你想要，就和我一起处理吧。我已经数完了这座城市中的绝大多数垃圾，只剩下新来的这三桶了。
t_en: If you want it, work with me. I have counted the majority of the trash in this city, leaving only these three newly arrived cans.

t_ch: 垃圾桶王国的垃圾会是什么样？抱着好奇心，你打开了第一个桶的盖子。
t_en: What sort of trash would Trashcan Kingdom hold? Curiosity piqued, you open the lid of the first trashcan.

t_ch: 可惜，没什么不同。也许爱德华医生没来得及去管这些细节。
t_en: Unfortunately, there isn't any difference. Maybe Dr. Edward did not have time to attend to these details.

t_ch: 这个垃圾桶里有本漫画，书页已经残破不堪。它讲述了这么一场冒险：塔塔洛夫曾是一位勇桶。他凭借勇气和意志击败了废品巨怪，还从它口中救出了一个人类。
t_en: There's a comic book inside this trashcan. The pages are torn and damaged. It tells of an adventure: Tatalov was once a brave trashcan. With valiance and willpower, it defeated a colossal junk monster and saved a human from its monstrous maw.

t_ch: 为了向塔塔洛夫报恩，那人类把自己也变成了垃圾桶…这竟然是个惊悚故事。
t_en: In order to repay Tatalov, that human turned himself into a trashcan... This is actually a horror story.

t_ch: 你看见了意料之外的东西，一只真蛰虫…它就这样躺在垃圾桶里，与你对视。
t_en: You see something unexpected — a True Sting, lying inside the trashcan, looking back at you.

t_ch: 「繁育」的余孽，去死吧！
t_en: Die, remnants of Propagation!

t_ch: 是时候为民除害了！用你的球棒告诉它，这里是「存护」的王国！
t_en: It is time to rid the people of this menace — Use your bat to tell it that this is a Kingdom of Preservation!

t_ch: 不错，年轻的桶，你证明了自己的智慧和耐心。你配得上这份「信任的证明」，我会将它转赠给你。
t_en: Not bad, young can. You've proven yourself by your wisdom and patience... You deserve this Proof of Trust. I'll gift it to you.

t_ch: 是谁打扰了我的沉眠？！
t_en: Who dares disrupt my slumber!?

t_ch: 一阵巨响过后，「繁育」的灾祸褪去，世界清净了。那位遭受无妄之灾的桶也回到了他的梦乡。谢谢你，无名客！
t_en: After a loud boom, Propagation's crisis has ended. The world is once again peaceful. And that trashcan who has endured undeserved ordeals has returned to his slumber. Thank you, Nameless!

t_ch: 我已年老体衰，无力与你并肩作战，但愿我的头脑能为你所用。
t_en: I'm now old and frail, no longer able to fight by your side. Hopefully, my mind can still be of use to you.

t_ch: 你赢得了我，还有其他桶的信任。希望此物可以表达我们对你的承认。
t_en: You've won me over, along with many others. This item carries our high regard for you.

t_ch: 你已经得到了我的信任。我将为你而战，伟大的灵魂。
t_en: You have earned my confidence. I will fight for you, O glorious soul.

t_ch: 我们都认可你的智慧与公平。
t_en: We all approve of your intellect and justness.

t_ch: 您用行动证明了自己。我们三桶将成为你的助力，为垃圾桶赢得自由。
t_en: You have proven yourself with your actions. We three cans shall become your aides, and win freedom once more for trashcans.

t_ch: 我们三桶将成为你的助力，为我等族类赢得自由。
t_en: We three cans will become your aides, and win freedom once more for our people.

t_ch: 等你向塔塔洛夫掀起反旗的那天，我和其他受压迫的桶必跟随你的左右。
t_en: When you raise the banners of rebellion against Tatalov, I, and all other oppressed cans will be at your side.

t_ch: 所以，袭击你的人是一位身穿红衣，擅长幻术，能变化成他人样貌的女性……
t_en: So, the one who attacked you was a girl dressed in red, adept at illusory arts, and was able to shapeshift...

t_ch: 她名叫「花火」，是混入匹诺康尼的假面愚者…放心，那姑娘暂时不会再打各位的主意了。她一定自以为得逞，正不知在何处得意洋洋呢。
t_en: Her name is Sparkle, a Masked Fool who has infiltrated Penacony... Don't worry. She won't be bothering you for now. She's probably patting herself on the back for a job well done.

t_ch: 你似乎很了解她。
t_en: It seems you know her well.

t_ch: 当然，我了解这里的每一个人。在匹诺康尼，所有人都可能说谎…但「记忆」不会。
t_en: Of course, I know everyone here. On Penacony, anyone could be lying... but the Remembrance does not lie.

t_ch: 所以我有必要向各位澄清一个事实：花火小姐的把戏能令人陷入幻觉，但那片诡异的梦境和她无关，而是源自匹诺康尼本身。
t_en: So I feel the need to clarify with everyone: Miss Sparkle's tricks can induce hallucinations, but that strange Dreamscape? Nothing to do with her — it originated from Penacony itself.

t_ch: 匹诺康尼…本身？
t_en: Penacony... itself?

t_ch: 还没意识到吗？所谓的「梦想之地」…其实是家族不知用何种手段精心维护的成果，一场设计好的美梦。
t_en: Have you not sensed it yet? This apparent land of dreams... It's a carefully designed dream maintained by The Family through unknown means.

t_ch: 而{M#他}{F#她}坠入的那片忆域——那才是梦境原本的样子，混乱、危险、神秘…变幻莫测的迷宫中栖息着记忆的{RUBY_B#忆域迷因}野兽{RUBY_E#}……
t_en: And the Memory Zone {M#he}{F#she} fell into, that's the true Dreamscape: Chaotic, dangerous, enigmatic... A {RUBY_B#Memory Zone Meme}beast{RUBY_E#} of memory dwells in the ever-shifting labyrinth...

t_ch: …她说的没错。各位，还记得入梦时的景色吗？
t_en: ...She's right. Everyone, do you all still remember your surroundings during your entry into the Dreamscape?

t_ch: 时钟…
t_en: Clocks...

t_ch: 鱼群…
t_en: Fish...

t_ch: 深海…
t_en: The deep sea...

t_ch: 咦，原来每个人看到的是一样的么？我记得…然后是一间客房，再通过镜子来到一条长长的走廊……
t_en: Huh, so everyone saw the same thing? I remember... it was a room, and then through a mirror, I came to a long hallway...

t_ch: 「思绪长廊」——酒店的服务人员是这么称呼它的。
t_en: The Gallery of Thoughts — that's how the hotel staff referred to it.

t_ch: 不觉得这些风景和梦中的匹诺康尼相去甚远…却和黑天鹅小姐描述的梦境十分相似么？
t_en: Have you noticed? These landscapes seem quite distinct from that of Penacony's in your dream, yet strikingly similar to the Dreamscape described by Miss Black Swan.

t_ch: 的确…初见时没放在心上，但回过头看，二者的共同点未免有些多了。
t_en: You're right... I didn't give it much thought at first, but in hindsight, the overlap is hard to ignore.

t_ch: 以及，我从一些熟客口中得知，入梦后的第一站原本是「梦中的酒店」，但因为一些意外…「酒店目前正在进行修缮」。
t_en: Also, after speaking to some of the frequenters, the first stop when you enter the dream is supposed to be the hotel in the dream, but due to certain incidents... the hotel is now under renovation.

t_ch: 请问一栋建筑在什么情况下才需要修缮？结合下{M#他}{F#她}方才的经历，答案很明了了。
t_en: What could possibly have to happen to an entire building for it to require renovation? Combine that with what {M#he}{F#she} just experienced, and the answer is clear.

t_ch: 匹诺康尼的美梦正在*沉没*，变回它原本的样子…坠入{RUBY_B#忆域}深海{RUBY_E#}。
t_en: Penacony's dream is *sinking* right now, turning back to its original form... and falling into the {RUBY_B#Memory Zone}deep sea{RUBY_E#}.

t_ch: 沉没？你的意思是…梦境世界正在瓦解？
t_en: Sinking? You mean... the Dreamscape is collapsing?

t_ch: 梦境酒店的遭遇就是预兆。
t_en: The encounter at the dreamscape Hotel was a sign.

t_ch: …原来如此，这样就能解释家族言语中的违和感了。
t_en: ...I see. That would explain the uneasiness we've sensed from The Family.

t_ch: 倘若这一消息被外界得知，无论住客们的实际安全如何，盛会之星的名誉必然会受到影响。谐乐大典将至，他们不得不隐瞒此事。
t_en: Leaving aside the question of guest safety, were this to become public, it would tarnish the name of the Planet of Festivities. With the Charmony Festival around the corner, it's no wonder they'd want to keep it under wraps.

t_ch: 住客们的安全，也许这一点也无法保障哦…{M#他}{F#她}遭遇的那只怪物不就是最好的证明吗？要我再提示一遍吗，它是忆域迷因……
t_en: Even the guests' safety cannot be assured now... Isn't the monster that {M#he}{F#she} encountered indisputable proof? Need I remind you that it is a Memory Zone meme...

t_ch: 暗喻死亡和谋杀的迷因怎会出现在家族宣称「绝对安全」的梦里…在匹诺康尼，所有人都可能说谎——或许也包括家族自己哦？
t_en: How could a meme that heralds death and murder appear in a dream that The Family proclaims "to be absolutely safe"... Everyone could be lying on Penacony — does that include The Family as well?

t_ch: 黑天鹅小姐，现状我们已充分了解了。回到你最初的提议……
t_en: Miss Black Swan, we fully understand our current predicament now. Going back to your original suggestion...

t_ch: 请问流光忆庭又是出于什么理由，选择和星穹列车合作呢？
t_en: For what reason has the Garden of Recollection chosen to work with the Astral Express?

t_ch: 嗯，请允许我纠正一下…这是我个人的请求，不代表忆庭。答案很简单，姬子小姐，我只是想做个交换…有关「记忆」的交换。
t_en: Hmm, let me rephrase then... this is my personal request, not the Garden of Recollection's. And the answer is simple. Miss Himeko, I simply wish to make an exchange... related to the Remembrance.

t_ch: 在职责之外，我也有些…个人的美学追求。我是忆者，也是一位收藏家，我也想见证那些晶莹、璀璨的宝贵记忆——这种想法很好理解，不是吗？
t_en: Apart from my duty, I also have some... personal pursuit in aesthetics. I am a Memokeeper, and a collector. I, too yearn to experience those luminous, dazzling, cherished memories. That's simple enough to understand, no?

t_ch: 而你们，星穹列车的无名客，「开拓」之道的践行者…我相信你们的潜质，也相信你们将在这片舞台上绽放出的…独一无二的光辉。
t_en: As for you all, Nameless of the Astral Express, and practitioners of the Trailblaze... I have faith in your potential, as well as in the... unique splendor you will unleash on the stage.

t_ch: 这就是我想和各位交朋友的原因。噩梦与美梦的交汇之地…作为「记忆」的摇篮再合适不过了。
t_en: This is why I wish to befriend all of you. A place where nightmares and sweet dreams intersect... the perfect cradle for the Remembrance.

t_ch: …我明白了。但在答复你之前，我们还需要做些讨论。
t_en: ...I understand. But before replying, we must first discuss this among ourselves.

t_ch: 当然，请随意。
t_en: Naturally, take your time.

t_ch: 咳咳，能听到吗？星穹列车，好久不见——匹诺康尼好玩吗？
t_en: Ahem, can you hear me? Astral Express, it's been a while. How's Penacony?

t_ch: 这是…是那个骇客小姑娘！
t_en: It's... It's that hacker girl!

t_ch: 说正经的。我知道各位正在四处调查匹诺康尼的异象，不如说，我们非常乐于看见这一幕。因为你们调查得越深入，就越有机会了解到美梦的真相。
t_en: I'll get straight to the point. I know you've all been investigating the abnormal phenomenon surrounding Penacony. Or rather, we are pleased to see that you've been doing that — because the deeper you delve, the closer you'll get to uncovering truth about this so-called "beautiful" dream.

t_ch: 家族正在隐瞒什么，不是吗？我们也掌握了一手情报。现在，我决定宽宏大量地把它与各位分享。只是，可惜——美好的假期要结束啦。
t_en: The Family is obviously hiding something, right? Well we've nabbed some valuable information, and out of the goodness of my heart I've decided to share it with all of you. Although, it unfortunately means your idyllic break in paradise is nearing its end.

t_ch: 把这段代码输进你们的入梦池，就是那个用来做梦的装置，到隐藏地图去看看吧！位置坐标都给你们打包好了，喏，一键复制就行。
t_en: Enter this string of code into your Dreampool — the device you use to dream with — and dive into the hidden map for a look! The coordinates have been sent to you. You just have to copy them over.

t_ch: 以及——想必你们已经听说萨姆的事了。好好期待一下吧，那家伙性格单纯，喜欢堂堂正正的胜负，一定与你们*合得来*。希望各位别被他的热情压垮！
t_en: Also, I take it you've already heard about Sam. You should look forward to the meeting. He's a straight shooter who values a fair fight. I think you'll *get along* with him just fine. Don't be overwhelmed by his enthusiasm!

t_ch: 哦，他让我转告各位：<color=#dbc291ff>无法抵达的梦中，剧目即将开演——</color>加油吧，各方势力都动起来了。无名客，别落后太远哦！
t_en: Oh, he had me relay a message to you: <color=#dbc291ff>The curtains have just risen over the dream that can't be reached</color>. The various factions have started to move. I wish you luck! Don't fall behind, Nameless!

t_ch: {NICKNAME}，你…没事吧？
t_en: {NICKNAME}, you... you all right?

t_ch: 刚才发生了什么？
t_en: What just happened?

t_ch: 这…是梦吗…
t_en: Is this... a dream...

t_ch: {NICKNAME}，看着我…冷静些，深呼吸，好吗？
t_en: {NICKNAME}, look at me... Calm down, deep breaths, all right?

t_ch: …我来吧，放轻松——你会没事的。
t_en: ...Allow me. Relax, you'll be fine.

t_ch: 放心，我没事。
t_en: Don't worry. I'm fine.

t_ch: 这…太突然了…
t_en: That... that was too sudden...

t_ch: 真的很抱歉，我得离开一小段时间…「死亡」还在游荡，我必须亲自去确认姬子小姐和三月七小姐的安危，并给予提醒……
t_en: I'm really sorry, but I have to leave for a short while... Death still hovers about, and I must personally ensure Miss Himeko and Miss March 7th's safety, and warn them...

t_ch: {NICKNAME}就拜托你了…黄泉小姐。
t_en: I'll leave {NICKNAME} to you... Miss Acheron.

t_ch: 发生这种事，我很遗憾…我太过专注于那女孩，却忽视了……
t_en: I'm sorry for what happened... I was too focused on that girl, and got sloppy...

t_ch: 是因为我的犹豫不决让她送了命…对不起，{NICKNAME}。
t_en: It was my hesitation that caused her to lose her life... I'm sorry, {NICKNAME}.

t_ch: 这不是你的错。
t_en: It's not your fault.

t_ch: 但如果那时，我选择拔刀的话……
t_en: But if in that moment, I chose to draw my blade...

t_ch: 你没有拔刀…为什么？
t_en: You didn't even draw your blade... Why...

t_ch: 对不起，我…别无选择。
t_en: Sorry, I... had no choice.

t_ch: 当务之急是弄清发生了什么。
t_en: The priority is figuring out what happened.

t_ch: 会的…我们会的。但时候未到。在「巡猎」之前，我们应当擦亮眼睛，思考真正的敌人藏身何处，以及如何与之相抗……
t_en: Yes... Yes, we will. But not yet. Before The Hunt, we should keep our eyes peeled and consider where the true enemies are hiding, and how to fight them...

t_ch: 她也没有选择…
t_en: She didn't have a choice either...

t_ch: 我明白你的愤怒，也愿意接受它…但时候未到。我们应当擦亮眼睛，思考真正的敌人藏身何处，以及如何与之相抗……
t_en: I understand your anger, and I will accept it... but this isn't the time. We should keep our eyes peeled and consider where the true enemies are hiding, and how to fight them...

t_ch: 不要让伤痛左右你的想法，{NICKNAME}。维持自我，你才能走在正确的路上。
t_en: Do not let pain dictate your thoughts, {NICKNAME}. Compose yourself, and you will walk the right path.

t_ch: 曾经，也有人这么告诫我…「对待敌人无需怜悯，那是对自己的残忍。但你必须要认清谁才是真正的敌人……」
t_en: Once, someone said to me: "No compassion for the enemy, for that is cruelty upon yourself. But you must see clearly who the true enemy is..."

t_ch: …以及，你要明白挥出那一刀的意义和代价。
t_en: ...And then, with one swing of your blade, you must understand its meaning and the price you pay.

t_ch: 这是一个身负累累血债之人能给你的唯一忠告。
t_en: This is the only advice I have for you, from someone burdened with a blood debt.

t_ch: ——二位，我回来了。
t_en: ...I'm back.

t_ch: {NICKNAME}，姬子小姐有话想对你说…有坏消息，也有好消息。
t_en: {NICKNAME}, Miss Himeko has something to tell you... There's good news, and bad news.

t_ch: 你们看着黄泉转身走去。你记得，她正是在那里化成了泡影。
t_en: You watch as Acheron turns away. You remember that it was there that she dissolved into a mere illusion.

t_ch: 你看见她停下脚步，只抿了抿唇，垂下双眼。半晌，她伛下身子，用手像是挽了些什么，复又起身。
t_en: You see her pause, lips pursed, eyes downcast. After a moment, she hunches down, as if gathering something with her hands, and rises again.

t_ch: 愿死亡结束你漫长的梦……
t_en: May death be the end of your boundless dream...

t_ch: 引领你归还…清醒的世界。
t_en: Guiding you back... to the waking world.

t_ch: 看——很符合常识，也很简单吧？
t_en: Look, it's common sense and easy, right?

t_ch: 我们…要从这里跳下去？
t_en: Are we... jumping down from here?

t_ch: 放心，我会稍加操作，将梦泡堆叠在一起，为各位安排一场舒适的软着陆。
t_en: Don't worry, I'll do something about it. Let me stack the dream bubbles to give everyone a nice, soft landing.

t_ch: 来吧，准备好……
t_en: Okay, here we go...

t_ch: 真的平安落地了。这还真是…神奇。
t_en: We landed safely. This is... amazing.

t_ch: 没有忆者陪同，不要擅自模仿哦。那么……
t_en: Don't imitate the Memokeeper without the accompaniment of one! Well...

t_ch: …也请这些闻风而来的好奇孩子打道回府吧。
t_en: ...wouldn't it be lovely if these nosy newcomers found their way back home?

t_ch: 对咯，我就喜欢这种大吃一惊的表情。
t_en: Right, I do enjoy seeing that shocked expression of yours.

t_ch: 朋友，现在让我们打开天窗说亮话吧，这个名叫「黄泉」的女人远没有你想象的那么简单，什么巡海游侠……
t_en: My friend, let's be honest. That woman named "Acheron" isn't as simple as you thought. Did she say she's a Galaxy Ranger?

t_ch: <color=#dbc291ff>——她是一位令使，她带来死亡和终局。</color>
t_en: <color=#dbc291ff>...She's actually an Emanator, who brings death and finality.</color>

t_ch: 黄泉确实有些奇怪，但…
t_en: Acheron has her peculiarities, but...

t_ch: 但是？没有什么但是，这就是事实。
t_en: But? There's no but. This is the truth.

t_ch: 令使？谁的令使？
t_en: An Emanator? Whose?

t_ch: 也许是「巡猎」…「毁灭」…甚至可能是「终末」，无所谓了，这些命途的其中一面——都指向同一种结果。
t_en: Perhaps The Hunt... or Destruction... or even Finality. It doesn't matter, these Paths all point to the same outcome.

t_ch: 不可能，我凭什么相信你？
t_en: Impossible. Why should I believe you?

t_ch: 不，黄泉就在现场，不会是她。
t_en: No way. Acheron was right there at the scene. It couldn't have been her.

t_ch: 现场？什么现场…不会吧…我的天——这下更有意思了。
t_en: She was there? Wait, what... You're kidding... Oh my, this just got even more interesting.

t_ch: 给你来点公司的料吧，朋友。你知道「冥火大公」——陀斐特的阿弗利特么？
t_en: Let me give you some IPC insider information, my friend. Do you know "Duke Inferno" — Fetora's Ifrit?

t_ch: 我和他交过手。
t_en: I fought him before.

t_ch: 可以啊，不愧是你！那就长话短说……
t_en: Nicely done, as expected! I'll cut to the chase then...

t_ch: 谁？
t_en: Who?

t_ch: 看来完全没印象啊…毕竟是泯灭帮，也算正常。那我就为你说明一下……
t_en: You don't seem to have heard of him before... He belongs to the Annihilation Gang, so I guess it's normal. Then let me explain to you...

t_ch: 这位冥火大公是来自陀斐特的火魔，一种元素生命，据说出身还和某位天才有点关系……
t_en: This Duke Inferno is a Fire Demon from Fetora. He's an energy lifeform, and it's rumored that his birth is related to a certain genius...

t_ch: 他和党羽组成「永火官邸」，视纳努克为恩主——实际是受这位大公领导，四处烧杀掳掠，践行「毁灭」的意志，甚至连其他泯灭帮也不放过。
t_en: He and his thugs formed "Ever-Flame Mansion" and viewed Nanook as their savior — in actuality, they were led by this duke to go around burning and looting, practicing the will of Destruction. And even the other Annihilation Gangs weren't spared.

t_ch: 也不知家族脑袋出了什么问题，又或者有人从中作梗，这帮家伙竟然也收到了邀请函。永火官邸当然不会拒绝，来势汹汹，誓要将盛会之星烧作一片火海……
t_en: I'm not sure what The Family was thinking, or perhaps someone was scheming behind the scenes, but shockingly these characters received an invitation. And why would the Ever-Flame Mansion decline? They harbor fierce intentions, swearing to turn the Planet of Festivities into a sea of blazing flames...

t_ch: 但不用担心，他们不会赴约了。你知道为什么吗？
t_en: But there's no need to worry. They won't be able to attend. Do you know why?

t_ch: ——因为阿弗利特死了。
t_en: Because Ifrit is dead.

t_ch: 怎么回事？
t_en: What happened?

t_ch: 不出所料…
t_en: As I expected...

t_ch: 他们在赴约途中覆灭了。凶手以一种神乎其技的方式将阿弗利特残忍杀害，劫走了泯灭帮收到的邀请函。永火官邸也分崩离析，作鸟兽散——
t_en: They were eliminated while on their way. The assailant, demonstrating remarkable expertise, ruthlessly ended Ifrit and swiped the invite that the Annihilation Gang had in their possession. The Ever-Flame Mansion disbanded, each going their separate ways...

t_ch: 而这之后，一位神秘的巡海游侠抵达匹诺康尼，靠一只八音盒入住了酒店…还需要我继续说下去吗，朋友？
t_en: Thereafter, a mysterious Galaxy Ranger arrived on Penacony, checking into the hotel with only a music box... Do you still need me to continue the story, my friend?

t_ch: 那人就是黄泉…
t_en: It was Acheron...

t_ch: 不排除其他人的可能。
t_en: It doesn't rule out others.

t_ch: 还有别的证据吗？
t_en: Do you have any other evidence?

t_ch: 巡海游侠神出鬼没，彼此之间也往来甚少，这件衣服太好穿了，只要她不松口，根本死无对证。公司是有办法追查，但也需要时间。
t_en: The Galaxy Rangers come and go like shadows, and there's little communication between them. Her story is almost too perfect. If she remains silent, there's no concrete evidence against her. While the IPC can investigate, it'll take some time.

t_ch: 所以，朋友，该你做出选择了…你可以现在，立马，头也不回地离开这里，永远放弃接近真相的机会。
t_en: So, my friend, the choice is yours now... You can leave this place immediately, without looking back, and forever forsake the chance to get closer to the truth.

t_ch: 与之相对地，你也可以接受我的邀请，并得知一个<color=#dbc291ff>事实</color>，一个足以颠覆匹诺康尼的事实。
t_en: Or, you can accept my invitation and learn a <color=#dbc291ff>truth</color>, a truth potent enough to upend everything in Penacony.

t_ch: 我需要你的帮助，所以会等你——但也不会等太久。准备好了…就跟上来吧。
t_en: I need your help, so I'll wait — but not for long. Once you are ready... follow me.

t_ch: 至于要不要和我合作，等看过那<color=#dbc291ff>事实</color>后再做决定…也不迟。
t_en: As for aligning with me, see the <color=#dbc291ff>truth</color> first, then decide... you'll still have time.

t_ch: 哥哥，我回来了。
t_en: Brother, I'm back.

t_ch: …欢迎回来。演出准备得如何了？
t_en: ...Welcome back. How's your preparation for the performance coming?

t_ch: 还好哦，放心。
t_en: It's fine. Don't worry.

t_ch: 「还好」？
t_en: "It's fine"?

t_ch: 嗯，这可不好…你是家族的骄傲，别让那些多余的情绪影响你完美的音韵。
t_en: Hmm, this is not good... You're the pride of The Family. Don't let those unnecessary emotions affect your perfect pitch.

t_ch: 我…知道啦。
t_en: I... know.

t_ch: 哥哥，你看起来有些消沉…发生什么事了？是那些收到「钟表匠」邀请的宾客吗？
t_en: Brother, you seem to be in low spirit... What's happened? Is it because of the Watchmaker's guest list?

t_ch: 是啊，我收到了报告…「死亡」带走了他们中的一些人。或许是受人指使。
t_en: Yes, I received the report that... "Death" had taken some of them. Perhaps someone was behind it.

t_ch: 啊，抱歉。我忘记你才刚回来，应该不知道这件事。
t_en: Ah, I'm sorry. I forgot you just came back. You probably wouldn't know about it.

t_ch: 不知从何时开始，名为「死亡」的梦魇在匹诺康尼降临，它对人进行无差别袭击，将精神的死亡平等地带给了所有人……
t_en: Somehow, a nightmare called "Death" has descended upon Penacony, striking indiscriminately, bringing spiritual death to all equally...

t_ch: 但家族构建的美梦中，任何不幸都不应发生。它严重破坏了梦境的秩序与和平…多么可恨。
t_en: In the utopian dreamscape envisioned by The Family, such sorrowful incidents shouldn't arise. It profoundly undermines the equilibrium and serenity of the dreamscape... How detestable.

t_ch: 竟然发生了这种事，那…是又有人遇害了吗？
t_en: I can't believe this has happened. Was... someone killed again?

t_ch: 嗯，共有两位。一位偷渡犯，以及……
t_en: Yes. There were two. One was a stowaway, the other...
...was you.

t_ch: 可以了，愚者。你的作为令我心寒。
t_en: That's enough Fool. Your deeds have saddened me.

t_ch: 你很敏锐嘛，鸡翅膀男孩。
t_en: Pretty sharp, aren't you, Chicken Wing Boy?

t_ch: 《谐乐颂》诚不我欺——「愚者的言语起头是愚昧，末尾是奸恶的狂妄」……
t_en: The Odes of Harmony talks about honesty — "The words of a fool begins with foolishness and end in treacherous arrogance"...

t_ch: …请回吧，祂的梦境不欢迎你。
t_en: ...Please leave. THEIR dreamscape doesn't welcome you.

t_ch: 哎呀，别板着脸嘛～还一本正经地引经据典，干嘛这么严肃？
t_en: Oh come on, lighten up! Why so serious with all the quotations and references?

t_ch: 我只是想问问——事到如今，家族还不打算出手吗？你那可怜的妹妹已经牺牲了哦？
t_en: I'm just curious — Now things have come to this, is The Family still unwilling to fight? I mean, your darling sister's already a goner, right?

t_ch: 你…难道就不想为她报仇吗？
t_en: Really? Don't tell me you're not craving a little vengeance.

t_ch: 尚不是时候。等到了命定的日子，我必按正直施行审判。
t_en: It's not yet time. When the fated day arrives, I shall mete out justice with unyielding righteousness.

t_ch: 厉害啊，这你都能忍～真是个冷血的家伙。嘿，我们说不定很聊得来哦？
t_en: Wow, you can endure that much? Truly, a heart of ice you've got there. Hey, maybe we could actually get along?

t_ch: 要不这样吧，我可以代你那位妹妹出席各种场合——你肯定也不想让人们知道…谐乐大典已经无法举办了吧？
t_en: How about this? I'll stand in for your dear sister at events — surely you don't want the world to hear the Charmony Festival's been called off?

t_ch: 家族自有安排。别再用你那诡诈的舌头玷污我亲爱的妹妹，愚者。
t_en: The Family has a plan, and do not dishonor my dear sister with your deceitful tongue again, Fool.

t_ch: 好吧好吧～我只是想说…如果你需要，我随时都可以帮你哦？谁能拒绝一位在鸡翅膀上打钉饰的男孩呢？
t_en: Alright, alright~ Just putting it out there... If you're ever in a pinch, remember I've got your back. I mean, who could resist a guy rocking spikes on his wings?

t_ch: 不必了，凶手已经在祂的光照下露了马脚。用不了多久，他便会因自己的计谋跌倒在地。
t_en: There's no need. The malefactor has been exposed under THEIR radiant gaze and will soon fall by their own machinations.

t_ch: 若他不回头，那祂的刀必磨快，弓必上弦，使恶人施加的毒害临到自己头上。
t_en: Should the transgressor fail to turn away from this path, THEIR sword will be honed, THEIR bow strung, causing the malevolence the perpetrator spreads to befall them.

t_ch: 等到那时，那不敬爱神的外邦人便知道自己不过是凡人，要坠落到阴间去……
t_en: When that time comes, the heathen will realize they are but a mere mortal, doomed to descend into the netherworld.

t_ch: …而我将成为祂的尖兵，亲自为你{RUBY_B#送葬}报喜{RUBY_E#}——「钟表匠」。
t_en: ...And I will join THEIR vanguard to announce this {RUBY_B#funeral}good news{RUBY_E#} to you personally, "Watchmaker."

t_ch: 姬子小姐，你看，我如约将这孩子带回来了。
t_en: Look, Miss Himeko: I brought the kid back as promised.

t_ch: {NICKNAME}，你没事吧！现实和梦里都找不到你，担心死我了……
t_en: {NICKNAME}, you're all right! We couldn't find you in either dream or reality, we were worried sick...

t_ch: 你平安无事就好。为你介绍一下——黑天鹅小姐，流光忆庭的忆者。
t_en: It's good to see you're unharmed. Allow me to introduce you to Miss Black Swan, Memokeeper of the Garden of Recollection.

t_ch: 你们已经认识了？
t_en: You know each other?

t_ch: 嗯。姬子姐姐说，她们是在调查梦境的过程中偶遇的。
t_en: Yeah. Miss Himeko said that they were investigating the incidents happening in the Dreamscape.

t_ch: 尚且只有一面之缘。但因为你，我们或许能够借此机会加深对彼此的了解呢。
t_en: We've only met once. But thanks to you, we might be able to use this opportunity to get to know each other a little bit better.

t_ch: 原来你是姬子的朋友。
t_en: So you're Himeko's friend.

t_ch: 哎呀，不是啦。姬子姐姐说，她们是在调查梦境的过程中偶遇的。
t_en: Oh, not exactly. Miss Himeko said that they were investigating the incidents happening in the Dreamscape.

t_ch: 目前还不是。但因为你，我们或许能够借此机会加深对彼此的了解呢。
t_en: Not quite yet. But thanks to you, we might be able to use this opportunity to get to know each other a little bit better.

t_ch: 所以，{NICKNAME}，你怎么会落入那种地方…发生了什么？
t_en: So, {NICKNAME}, how did you end up there... and what the heck happened?

t_ch: （告知此前的经历…）
t_en: (Elaborate on what you experienced...)

t_ch: …姬子，你怎么看？
t_en: ...How do you see things, Himeko?

t_ch: 她的话不可尽信，有不少刻意诱导的成分。
t_en: It's best to be skeptical of her. There's a significant amount of intentional misdirection in her words.

t_ch: 但我会担心最坏的可能，假如梦境的异变确实存在，并且不是自然发生，而是有人在幕后推动……
t_en: But I worry about the worst outcome: If the Dreamscape's anomaly really exists, and it isn't a natural occurrence, but instigated by someone behind the scenes...

t_ch: 那么它大概率和「钟表匠的邀请函」有关。
t_en: ...Then it likely has something to do with the Watchmaker's invite.

t_ch: 咦，这是怎么推断出来的？
t_en: Huh, how'd you figure that one out?

t_ch: 暂且不考虑极端情况，推动梦境异变的主使，立场定然与家族对立，那就不外乎两种可能……
t_en: Well, excluding any extreme scenarios, the mastermind responsible for these shifts in the dreamscape undeniably stands against The Family, which narrows it down to two possibilities...

t_ch: 有人意图引入外部势力，借机动摇家族对匹诺康尼的掌控；或是家族为了自保，被迫暗中向外界求援。
t_en: Someone is drawing in external influences to distract and disrupt The Family's control over Penacony. Either that, or The Family has been forced to seek outside aid surreptitiously for self-preservation.

t_ch: 但从邀请函的密文，和家族的反应来看…前者的可能性更高些。
t_en: But from the encrypted message on the invite, and The Family's reaction... The former is more likely.

t_ch: 这也就意味着，发出无名客密文的人和梦境异变的幕后主使是同一阵营…甚至可能是同一个人。
t_en: Which also means that, the one who sent out the encrypted Nameless message and the mastermind behind the anomaly are on the same side... Maybe even the same person.

t_ch: 但…这也不说明什么吧。只谈手法，愚者和谜语人也做得到，公司不也破译成功了吗？
t_en: Well... this doesn't necessarily mean a thing, right? In terms of methods, both the Fools and the Riddlers could pull it off. Hasn't the IPC cracked the message, too?

t_ch: 小三月，别紧张，这只是一种可能性。可如果这行密文不是单纯的恶作剧，而是什么人有意邀请「开拓」入场，我们就更没有理由坐视不管。
t_en: March, calm down. It's just a possibility. But if this encrypted message isn't some harmless prank, but done so to invite the Trailblaze into the fray, we cannot remain uninvolved.

t_ch: 那来说说我的发现吧——很遗憾，是个坏消息。
t_en: Well, let me tell you what I discovered, and I'm sorry — it's bad news.

t_ch: 据可靠消息称，一些人在匹诺康尼目击到了身穿银色盔甲的高大男性。我向猎犬家系打听了消息，也走访了不少声称见过这位入侵者的来宾……
t_en: According to reliable sources, some people spotted a tall silver-armored man on Penacony. I've reached out to the Bloodhound Family for news, and visited many of those guests who claimed to have seen this invader...

t_ch: …然后收到了这个。{NICKNAME}，我发给你了，打开看看吧。
t_en: ...And this is what I received. {NICKNAME}, I've sent it to you. Open it and take a look.

t_ch: 这群星核猎手…竟然在匹诺康尼也有布局，真是纠缠不休！
t_en: Those Stellaron Hunters... They even factored Penacony into their plan! Nowhere's safe from them!

t_ch: 形势很明朗了。盛会之星远没有表面上看起来那么简单，围绕着「钟表匠的遗产」，包含家族在内的一众势力各怀目的，彼此制衡。
t_en: The situation is clear — the Planet of Festivities isn't what it seems on the surface. Everyone's sights, including the factions in The Family, are set on the Watchmaker's Legacy. Every party has its own motives, and they're attempting to thwart each other.

t_ch: 尽管还不知道这遗产是什么，但这场争夺战波及无辜群众…是早晚的事。
t_en: While we still don't know what this legacy entails, it's only a matter of time before the power struggle ensnares innocent bystanders.

t_ch: 「熔火骑士」萨姆，据称此人是格拉默铁骑的余党，先天的基因改造战士，认知异于常人，行事决绝、不留余地，是不亚于卡芙卡和刃的危险分子。
t_en: The Molten Knight, Sam, is reportedly a remnant of Glamoth's Iron Cavalry — a genetically engineered warrior. He possesses superhuman perception and acts decisively — he'll leave no margin for error. This individual is every bit as dangerous as Kafka and Blade.

t_ch: 这是一封威胁信。银狼口中的隐藏地图想必就是封锁中的梦境酒店。为后续考虑，我们确实有必要拜访一下现场。
t_en: This message is a threat. The hidden map that Silver Wolf mentioned is probably the sealed dreamscape Hotel. For the sake of what's to come, a visit seems necessary.

t_ch: 要通知家族吗？
t_en: Should we inform The Family?

t_ch: 虽然态度友好，但他们未必信任我们…先行后闻吧。
t_en: We may be on amicable terms, but that doesn't mean they trust us... Let's act first and talk later.

t_ch: 被发现了怎么办…
t_en: What if we get found out...

t_ch: 也许是星核猎手的陷阱…
t_en: It could be a Stellaron Hunter's trap...

t_ch: 所以我没有第一时间提出，但现在看来，星穹列车有入场的理由。
t_en: Which is why I didn't suggest it initially. But the way things are now, the Astral Express has a reason to join the fray.

t_ch: 如果家族问起，就如实告知列车组在追查星核猎手，相信能得到理解。根据对方的反应，我们再采取下一步行动。
t_en: If The Family asks, just tell them that the Express is investigating the Stellaron Hunters — I'm sure they'll understand. We'll plan our next move according to how they react.

t_ch: 看来我们达成共识了——那就回去通知黑天鹅小姐吧。
t_en: Then we are in agreement. Let's return and inform Miss Black Swan.

t_ch: 黑天鹅小姐，列车组愿意与你合作。在忆域中，我们需要一位合适的向导。
t_en: Miss Black Swan, the Express is willing to cooperate with you — we need a proper guide to navigate the Memory Zone.

t_ch: 听凭差遣，不会让各位失望的。
t_en: I'm at your service. I will not disappoint.

t_ch: {NICKNAME}和小三月，回到各自的房间吧。准备入梦，如果顺利…我们在梦境酒店的大堂集合。
t_en: {NICKNAME} and March, return to your own rooms. Enter the dream, and if everything goes without a hitch... we'll see one another at the dreamscape Hotel's lobby.

t_ch: 我会留在现实，确保各位安全无虞。如有需要，也会出面与家族交涉——没问题吧，黑天鹅小姐？
t_en: I'll stay in the real world to ensure everyone's safety. If needed, I will also parlay with The Family. I assume there's no problem, Miss Black Swan?

t_ch: 看来即便我亲手拯救了你们的同伴，各位也还是很难信任我呢…当然没问题，只是有点伤害我的感情。
t_en: It seems that I'll have a hard time gaining your trust even if I personally did rescue your friends... It's no problem, of course, other than the fact that my feelings are a little hurt.

t_ch: 拜托你了，瓦尔特。至于我们…就准备好一睹「梦境的真容」吧。
t_en: We're counting on you, Welt. As for us... Let's get ready to see the Dreamscape's true visage.

t_ch: 记忆告诉你，这里是三月七的房间。不出意外她会在这里「苏醒」。
t_en: Your memories tell you this is March 7th's room. Unless fate intervenes, she will "awaken" here.

t_ch: 三月七，你在里面吗？
t_en: March, are you there?

t_ch: 你敲了敲门。她没有回应。
t_en: You knock, but there's no response...

t_ch: 你在吗？我们要出发了。
t_en: Are you in there? We are about to set off.

t_ch: 你再次敲了敲门。她没有回应。
t_en: You knock again, but there's no response...

t_ch: 三月？是我，{NICKNAME}…
t_en: March? It's me, {NICKNAME}...

t_ch: 你用力敲了敲门。她依旧没有回应。
t_en: You knock harder, and there's still no response.

t_ch: 我知道你在想什么，很可惜…那姑娘不在里面，这里也不是她的房间。
t_en: I know what you're thinking, but alas... That girl isn't in there, and neither is this her room.

t_ch: 从你踏入忆域的那一刻起，现实的建筑构造便没有意义了。我能感受到，此刻她在很远的地方。
t_en: The moment you stepped into the Memory Zone, reality's building structures lost all meaning. I can sense that she is in a faraway place right now.

t_ch: 同样地，要找到领航员小姐所说的大堂…我们也得多花些心思。
t_en: In the meantime, if we want to find that lobby the Navigator mentioned... we'll have to try a little harder.

t_ch: 你看起来很紧张啊，{NICKNAME}。是在担心那个小姑娘吗？
t_en: You look anxious, {NICKNAME}. Worried about that girl?

t_ch: 如果有心事，不妨向我倾诉吧。这世上可没有比忆者更适合做心理治疗师的存在了。
t_en: You can talk to me if you've something on your mind. In this world, there's no one who heals the heart better than a Memokeeper.

t_ch: 感谢你的救命之恩。
t_en: Thank you for saving me.

t_ch: 别客气，这是我应该做的。毕竟你很特殊…特殊到有许多双眼睛在注视着你。
t_en: I just did what I had to do. You are special, after all... So special that many pairs of eyes are watching your every move.

t_ch: 你能确认流萤的安危吗？
t_en: Can you confirm Firefly is safe?

t_ch: 放心吧，那是一道单向门，只会通往清醒的现实。除非那女孩抱有强烈的意愿，怎么也不肯从梦中醒来…怎么可能呢？她现在一定正在酒店的某处揉眼睛呢。
t_en: Don't worry. That is a one-way door leading to awakened reality. Unless that girl is so stubborn that she refuses to wake up from the dream — which isn't possible — she must be holed up somewhere in the hotel massaging her eyes right now.

t_ch: 说实话，我不太信任忆者。
t_en: To be honest, I don't really trust Memokeepers.

t_ch: 看来你有些不太愉快的回忆呢…但显然，接下来的合作对你、对我、对列车组都是明智的，对吧？所以你可以安心地依赖我哦。
t_en: It appears you've had some unhappy memories... But it's obvious that this partnership makes the most sense to you, me, and the Express Crew, no? So you can take it easy and trust me.

t_ch: 当然，前来这里的忆者不止我一人——匹诺康尼首次向家族以外的派系公开发出邀约，忆庭要铭记下这历史性的一刻……
t_en: Of course, I'm not the only Memokeeper who has come — Penacony has opened its doors to factions beyond The Family for the first time, and the Garden must record this historic moment...

t_ch: 所以你也可以试着和别人合作哦…只要你看得见他们，呵呵。
t_en: So you can try working together with others as well... As long as you can see them, hehe.

t_ch: 你不回房间吗？
t_en: You're not returning to your room?

t_ch: 不呀。我要陪你们一起入梦，免得你们受伤。反正忆者也不需要什么入梦池…对吧？
t_en: No. I will accompany you into the dream to prevent harm from befalling you all. Memokeepers don't need a Dreampool anyway.

t_ch: 就聊到这里吧。
t_en: That's all for now.

t_ch: 好呀～那就安心去睡吧，我会好好陪着你的。
t_en: All right. Drift peacefully off to sleep, and I'll be right with you.

t_ch: （回到熟悉的房间了……）
t_en: (Back in this familiar room...)

t_ch: 这边，亲爱的。
t_en: This way, darling.

t_ch: 怎么样，身体没有不舒服吧？
t_en: What's wrong? Are you not feeling well?

t_ch: 感觉还好。
t_en: I feel fine.

t_ch: 那我就安心了。
t_en: That's good then.

t_ch: 感觉很难受…
t_en: I don't feel so good...

t_ch: 我知道你对忆质比较敏感，比其他人更容易受到梦境的影响，这也是我需要特别陪同你的原因。
t_en: I know you're more sensitive to memoria, thus more easily affected by the Dreamscape than others. This is also why I have to accompany you.

t_ch: 我会略施手段让你不那么难受。别担心，你的伙伴也很安全，我在入梦前向她们两人各要了一件小首饰，这能让我在忆域中感应她们的存在。
t_en: I shall use my methods to relieve you. Don't worry, as your companions are safe — I had both of them each give me a trinket before I entered the dream. This will allow me to feel their presence in the Memory Zone.

t_ch: 她们已在各自的房间里醒来了。这下，你应该就能放宽心跟我来了吧？时间不等人，这就出发去大堂吧。
t_en: They're already awake in their rooms. Will you be comfortable in following me now? Time waits for no one. We must hurry to the lobby.

t_ch: 这里…难道没有工作人员吗？
t_en: Are there... no staff members here at all?

t_ch: 咦，怎么是你们？
t_en: Huh? What're you doing here?

t_ch: 你…又迷路了？
t_en: You... lost your way again?

t_ch: 也不能这么说吧，我只是不知道自己在哪里。
t_en: Well, not really. I just don't know where I am.

t_ch: 你们…认识？
t_en: You... know each other?

t_ch: 嗯，她就是我提到的那位忆者。
t_en: Yes. She is the Memokeeper I mentioned.

t_ch: 两位，寒暄暂且到此为止吧…我们先合力请走这些好客的孩子，如何？
t_en: We'll have to cut the banter short... Let's first work together and remove these overly friendly children, shall we?

t_ch: 不胜荣幸，那就感谢二位帮忙了。
t_en: I'm indebted to you both for your help.

t_ch: 总算安静了。两位为什么会出现在这里？
t_en: Finally, some peace and quiet. What are the two of you doing here?

t_ch: 事实上，这也是我们想问你的问题。
t_en: We could ask you the same thing.

t_ch: <color=#ff3a3e>说来话长，我因机缘巧合——{M#他}{F#她}应该记得——与猎犬家系的一部分人成了朋友，正在帮助他们追查要犯…不知怎的，就误入了这里。</color>
t_en: <color=#ff3a3e>It's a long story. I fortuitously — as {M#he}{F#she} should remember — became friends with some of the Bloodhounds, and was helping them track down a wanted criminal... Except I ended up here for some reason.</color>

t_ch: <color=#ff3a3e>或许没有你们想的那么复杂，这全部归功于…一位假面愚者。也许是我的无心之言伤害了她…在一阵五光十色后，我就身处此地了。</color>
t_en: <color=#ff3a3e>It's probably not as complicated as it sounds. It's all thanks to... a Masked Fool. Maybe I unwittingly hurt her... and then in a flash of kaleidoscopic light, I ended up here.</color>

t_ch: <color=#ff3a3e>匹诺康尼坊间流传着一些飞语，涉及钟表匠、遗产、噩梦等隐秘的话题。我好奇是谁在散布消息，就照着流言中的方法试验了下…竟真的来到了这里。</color>
t_en: <color=#ff3a3e>Penacony's rife with rumors now, about the Watchmaker, a legacy, nightmares and other mysterious tales. I was curious as to who was spreading them, and tested the method as described in the rumors... To think I actually ended up here...</color>

t_ch: 你…应该没有隐瞒些什么吧？
t_en: You... aren't hiding anything, are you?

t_ch: 隐瞒？在一位忆者面前，我应该做不到这事。
t_en: Hiding? I don't think I'd be capable of that in front of a Memokeeper.

t_ch: 的确。
t_en: That's true.

t_ch: 我就能做到。
t_en: I can.

t_ch: 是吗…那你很厉害，是受过什么训练吗，是否可以教教我？
t_en: Is that so... You're good then. What sort of training did you have to undergo? Can you teach me?

t_ch: 开玩笑的，{M#他}{F#她}做不到。
t_en: {M#He}{F#She}'s kidding. {M#He}{F#She} can't do it.

t_ch: 在我面前就可以吗？
t_en: How about in front of me?

t_ch: 不，我不是那个意思，这只是一种…修辞。
t_en: That's not what I meant. It's just a... figure of speech.

t_ch: 两位来得正巧，这片梦境危机四伏。既然我们目的一致，不如结伴同行吧。
t_en: Your arrival is timely — this Dreamscape is fraught with danger. Since our destination is the same, how about we join forces?

t_ch: 我可以保护你们免受伤害，在忆域中，我也需要忆者的援助，以及…如果遇见家族，多一个人总能多一份解释。
t_en: I can protect you from harm, but in the Memory Zone, I'll need assistance from the Memokeeper as well. And... if we run into The Family, it wouldn't hurt to have one more person to explain the situation.

t_ch: 这…你怎么看，{NICKNAME}？
t_en: Hmm... What do you think, {NICKNAME}?

t_ch: 人多一点不是坏事。
t_en: Never hurts to have more people.

t_ch: 可以相信黄泉。
t_en: We can trust Acheron.

t_ch: 还是算了吧…
t_en: I think we should forget it...

t_ch: 抱歉…我有点没听清，可以再说一遍吗？
t_en: Sorry. I couldn't hear you clearly. Could you repeat that?

t_ch: 我的意思是，没问题。
t_en: I mean, no problem.

t_ch: 我的意思是，不行…
t_en: I mean, no can do...

t_ch: 抱歉…我有点没听清，可以再说一遍吗？
t_en: Sorry. I couldn't hear you clearly. Could you repeat that?

t_ch: 我的意思是，没问题。
t_en: I mean, no problem.

t_ch: <color=#ff3a3e>我的意思是，没问题。</color>
t_en: <color=#ff3a3e>I mean, no problem.</color>

t_ch: （怎么回事，那种微妙的感觉又来了……）
t_en: (What's going on? That weird feeling is back again...)

t_ch: 不，绝对不可以。
t_en: No, absolutely not.

t_ch: 抱歉…我有点没听清，可以再说一遍吗？
t_en: Sorry. I couldn't hear you clearly. Could you repeat that?

t_ch: 我的意思是，没问题。
t_en: I mean, no problem.

t_ch: 我的意思是，不行。
t_en: I mean, no can do.

t_ch: 抱歉…我有点没听清，可以再说一遍吗？
t_en: Sorry. I couldn't hear you clearly. Could you repeat that?

t_ch: 我的意思是，没问题。
t_en: I mean, no problem.

t_ch: <color=#ff3a3e>我的意思是，没问题。</color>
t_en: <color=#ff3a3e>I mean, no problem.</color>

t_ch: （怎么回事，那种微妙的感觉又来了……）
t_en: (What's going on? That weird feeling is back again...)

t_ch: 谢谢你，{NICKNAME}…真的很感谢。
t_en: Thank you, {NICKNAME}... I'm truly grateful.

t_ch: 也很高兴能再次与你同行，黑天鹅小姐。
t_en: Glad to be traveling with you again, Miss Black Swan.

t_ch: 嗯…但愿如此。
t_en: Mm... I truly hope so.

t_ch: 这扇门…打不开吗？
t_en: This door... can't be opened?

t_ch: 上锁了，我倒是可以随意穿行，但你们……
t_en: It's locked. I can travel through it freely, but you all...

t_ch: 只能走我来时的路了。二位，请跟我来吧。
t_en: We'll just have to take the way I came. Follow me.

t_ch: 这里…也走不通。
t_en: This way... is blocked as well.

t_ch: 这间房间…怎么上下颠倒过来了？
t_en: This room... Why is it upside down?

t_ch: 忆域的样貌无时无刻不在变幻，看来我们得想办法…走到天花板上。
t_en: The Memory Zone is constantly shifting its form. Looks like we'll have to think of a way... to walk on the ceiling.

t_ch: 我们现在就在天花板上。
t_en: We're standing on the ceiling right now.

t_ch: …黄泉小姐的话不无道理。无论如何，我们得登上悬在头顶的那个平面——当然，绝不是抱着柱子上去。
t_en: ...Miss Acheron's words speak sense. Regardless, we have to reach that flat surface hanging overhead. And we're not scaling the pillar, of course.

t_ch: 我来为各位传授一些在忆域中行动的小技巧吧。
t_en: I'll teach everyone some traversal tricks used in the Memory Zone.

t_ch: 这扇门…也上锁了吗？
t_en: This door... is locked too?

t_ch: 这样下去没完没了。还是让我来吧。
t_en: There's no end to this. Let me try.

t_ch: 你看到忆者合上双眼。她在以一种近乎感应的方式搜索着房间，甚至房间以外的每一处角落。
t_en: You see the Memokeeper close her eyes. She is searching the room in an almost telepathic way, even every nook and cranny outside the room.

t_ch: 这片忆域…有点过于扭曲了。我得采用些不那么优雅的手段，二位…请给我一点时间……
t_en: This Memory Zone... is overly twisted. I have to use inelegant methods, so you two... please give me a little time...

t_ch: 嗯…有了。我能看见这片梦境的中心，还有家族的人…还有几个身影在摸索着向前，看来你的朋友也不太顺利呢…一个、两个、三个……
t_en: Mm... got it. I can see the core of this Dreamscape, and there are members of The Family... and some figures trying to find their way forward. It appears that your friends aren't faring too well... One, two, three...

t_ch: …不对，三个？还有第三个人在寻找去大堂的路？等等…这是……
t_en: ...Wait, three? There is a third person seeking the way to the lobby? Wait... it's...

t_ch: ——是之前和你在一起的小姑娘？
t_en: ...The girl who was with you?

t_ch: 流萤？她为什么在这里？
t_en: Firefly? What's she doing here?

t_ch: 会不会是认错了？
t_en: Could you be mistaken?

t_ch: 你说她已经回到现实了。
t_en: You said she already returned to reality.

t_ch: 我不明白，但忆域中有熟悉的影子，和她给人的感觉很相似…她有什么一定要深入梦境的理由？
t_en: It eludes me, but there's a known semblance in the Memory Zone, mirroring the essence she radiates... What reason does she have to delve so deep into the Dreamscape?

t_ch: 她…是在奔跑吗？不…奔逃？她的身后似乎有什么……
t_en: She... Is she running? No... Running away? Something's right behind her...

t_ch: …不好。各位，我们得加速了。
t_en: ...This is not good. Everyone, we must hurry.

t_ch: 情况紧急，我只能破例用些手段，带你们一同穿过忆域。
t_en: There's no time. I'll just have to break the rules and use some methods to help you phase through the Memory Zone.

t_ch: 我从忆域中采撷了几缕她的思绪，这能帮助你们对她建立印象。现在，牢牢抓住这些思绪，在脑海中把它们整理成型。
t_en: I picked up slivers of her thoughts in the Memory Zone. These will help you form an impression of her. Now, you must hang on to these thoughts with all your might, and shape them in your mind.

t_ch: 刚才，我捕捉到了一些非常熟悉的记忆，出现在这里的*老朋友*不仅有她，还有你们遇险时，现场的第三者……
t_en: I caught some very familiar memories just now — she's not the only *old friend* who has appeared here. There was also a third party at the scene when you ran into danger...

t_ch: ——那只忆域迷因，它也在那里。
t_en: ...That Memory Zone meme, it is here as well.

t_ch: 闸门关闭了。你感觉自己的思绪也被回旋的水流扰动，牵引其中，在深谷中下降、沉沦。当舱门再度开启时，你会看到何种景象？也许没人能给出答案。
t_en: The gate shuts. Your thoughts, stirred by the swirling current and pulled along, descend into a deep abyss, sinking. When the hatch opens again, what sights will you behold? Perhaps no one can provide an answer.

t_ch: 某种难以名状的流质——黑暗——自你的胸膛漫向喉头。窒息感自内而外将你淹没。然后，声音响起……
t_en: An indescribable fluid — darkness — flows from your chest to your throat. Suffocation engulfs you from within. Then, a voice emerges...

t_ch: 「我梦见一片焦土，一株破土而生的新蕊，它迎着朝阳绽放，向我低语呢喃。」
t_en: In my dream, I saw a scorched earth, and a new sapling emerging from it. It bloomed against the morning sun, and whispered to me.

t_ch: 人们为何选择沉睡？我想……
t_en: Why do people choose to sleep? I think...

t_ch: 最后的轻叹不知是谁发出的。
t_en: The final sigh escapes, its origin unknown.

t_ch: …是因为害怕从「梦」中醒来。
t_en: ...It is because they're afraid to awaken from the dream.

t_ch: …小心！附近有危险的气息。
t_en: ...Watch out! A dangerous presence lurks nearby.

t_ch: …各位，保持警惕。
t_en: ...Everyone, be on your guard.

t_ch: 忆者递出一枚忆泡，示意你们用额头抵住它。你照做了，一道刺骨的寒意直入躯壳，随后聚成清晰的影像。
t_en: The Memokeeper hands over a Memory Bubble, signaling for you to press it against your forehead. You do as instructed, and a bone-chilling coldness pierces your body, which then consolidates into a clear image.

t_ch: 你看到姬子和三月七在一起，被家族拦住了去路。黑天鹅就在她们身侧，但家族并未注意到她。
t_en: You see Himeko and March 7th together, obstructed by The Family. Black Swan stands beside them, unnoticed by the The Family.

t_ch: 「状况我了解了。请你务必带{NICKNAME}撤离到安全地点。如果有余裕，也可先行调查流萤一事。稍后在『钟表小子』雕像处会合。」
t_en: I understand the situation now. Please take {NICKNAME} and retreat to a safe location. If time allows, you can investigate Firefly's matter first. Meet later at Clockie's statue.

t_ch: 忆泡中的记忆到此为止了。
t_en: Memories in the bubbles come to an end here.

t_ch: 所以，我要受姬子小姐之托，带两位返回现实了。{NICKNAME}…很高兴看到你一切安好，我们出发吧，到安全的地方再谈。
t_en: Which is why I must undertake Miss Himeko's request and bring both of you back to reality. {NICKNAME}... I'm glad to see that you're fine. Let us reach a safe place before speaking again.

t_ch: 黑天鹅小姐，你不打算开个…传送门，之类的吗？
t_en: Miss Black Swan, do you not intend to open some sort of... teleporter?

t_ch: 嗯…我不建议这么做。{M#他}{F#她}的精神状况尚不稳定，得尽量避免刚才那种*粗暴*的移动方式。
t_en: Mm... I don't recommend that. {M#His}{F#Her} mental state is unstable, so we have to avoid any *rough* traversal methods like just now.

t_ch: 以及——在撤离的同时，我们还能为流萤小姐做更多事，不是吗？这附近还残存着一些她留下的痕迹。在它们彻底消失前，让其成为你们的「记忆」吧。
t_en: Also, while we're departing, we can still do more for Miss Firefly, can't we? Some remnants of her presence linger nearby. Seize the chance to etch them into your "Remembrance" before they're gone.

t_ch: …如果能顺路开展调查，也好。
t_en: ...If we can start investigating on the way, that'd be good.

t_ch: 出发吧，我们得另寻他路……
t_en: Let's go. We have to find another path...

t_ch: 抱歉，可以再给我几分钟吗？
t_en: Sorry, but could you give me a few more minutes?

t_ch: 我…还有件未完成的事。
t_en: I... have some unfinished business.

t_ch: 「钟表匠的遗产」……
t_en: Watchmaker's Legacy...

t_ch: 我…不想放弃……
t_en: I... won't give up...

t_ch: 我有这个权利…对吧……
t_en: I have this right... Don't I...

t_ch: 这里…到处都是烧焦的残骸？
t_en: These are all... charred remains?

t_ch: 还残留着些微火星，始作俑者大概率还没走远。
t_en: They're still smoldering — the culprit can't have gotten far.

t_ch: 看来我们得暂时放慢脚步了……
t_en: I suppose we can slow down for now then...

t_ch: 尽是些*燃烧*的记忆…在这片深海中尤为突出呢。
t_en: They're all just *burning* Remembrances... a stark contrast to this deep sea.

t_ch: 小心，这些痕迹是刚留下的，那位不速之客还没走远。
t_en: Careful, these traces are fresh. Our unsought guest isn't far away.

t_ch: 这只怪物的残骸表面切口窄而深，周围明显有剧烈灼烧形成的焦黑痕迹。几点星火在其中飞舞跃动，像是逐{RUBY_B#「虚无」的星神}Ⅸ{RUBY_E#}而去的破碎恒星。
t_en: The remains of this creature bears narrow and deep incisions, with evident traces of charring inflicted by intense burning. A few sparks dance and flicker within, resembling shattered stars departing towards {RUBY_B#Aeon of Nihility}IX{RUBY_E#}.

t_ch: （看来这只怪物死于某种热能武器……）
t_en: (Looks like this monster was slain by a thermic weapon...)

t_ch: 这只怪物的残骸被某种利器洞穿，其外壳下的组织似乎在短时间内因高热而爆发性地尽数蒸发。你不能再靠得更近——那自残骸体内寻隙而出的蒸汽毫无疑问会灼伤你。
t_en: The remains of this creature, pierced by an unknown weapon, reveal inner tissues that have been seemingly evaporated rapidly due to intense heat. You cannot approach any closer — the steam escaping from the shattered body would undoubtedly scorch you.

t_ch: （看来这只怪物死于某种高热反应……）
t_en: (Looks like this monster died of a hyperthermic reaction...)

t_ch: 这只怪物的残骸表面似乎没有任何异样。但当你以手抚之，指尖传来的高热令你如触电般退却。
t_en: The surface of this creature's remains appears unremarkable. Yet, as your hand grazes it, the searing heat transferred to your fingertips sends you jolting back, as if struck by an electric shock.

t_ch: 毫无疑问，残骸内部现在正有一团剧烈焚烧的火焰亟待破壳而出。为了你的生命安全，你最好离它远点。
t_en: Without a doubt, a fierce inferno rages within the remains, desperately longing to break free from its confines. For the sake of your safety, it is best to keep your distance.

t_ch: 不对劲。这些痕迹都是前不久留下的，有人刚从这里离开…会和方才的事有关么？
t_en: Something's off. These traces are fresh. Someone just left the scene... Would this person have anything to do with what happened just now?

t_ch: 仅凭几条线索，我也很难做出推断。
t_en: I cannot reach a conclusion with so few clues.

t_ch: 听听你的直觉，毕竟你说过…「记忆」不会说谎。
t_en: Just listen to your gut — you were the one who said that... the Remembrance doesn't lie.

t_ch: 从这些残骸的记忆来看…那人身材高大，比成年男性还要健壮许多，手段干脆利落，都是一击毙命——也许是雇佣兵，或者杀手。
t_en: From the memories of these carcasses... that person was tall, and much stronger than an adult male. The method was clean and crisp, all one-strike kills — maybe a mercenary, or an assassin.

t_ch: 他是从大堂那一侧的门进来的，朝着酒店更深处前进了，既然如此…他应该看到了此前发生的一切……
t_en: He entered through the door from the lobby, and went farther into the hotel. If that's the case... he should have seen everything that happened here...

t_ch: …收回前言。我们得尽快赶去那片烙印了流萤小姐身姿的忆域。以这位朋友的做事风格……
t_en: ...I take back what I said. We have to hurry to that Memory Zone where Miss Firefly's presence was imprinted. Considering what this person can do...

t_ch: 如果他的目的地也是那里——现场就不会留下任何线索了。
t_en: If his goal is that place as well, there won't be anything left there.

t_ch: 这可真是…「灿烂夺目」。就是这个房间吗？
t_en: This is truly... a magnificent sight. It's this room, then?

t_ch: 是。准确地说——在正下方。
t_en: Yes. More precisely, it's right below.

t_ch: 「正下方」？
t_en: Right below?

t_ch: 就是字面意思，正下方。
t_en: Exactly what it means: Down.

t_ch: 二位虽然不能像忆者一样直接穿过物理结构，但我想到一个好办法，能让你们安全无虞地抵达这个房间的正下层。
t_en: You two may not be able to phase through physical structures like Memokeepers, but I've got a great idea that will let you safely reach the floor right below this room.
